answer: The capital city of France is Paris, which is also its largest city and main cultural and economic center. Paris is renowned for its rich history, iconic landmarks, and significant role in fashion and art.

results.title: Paris 🇫🇷, the capital and largest city of France, is located in ...
results.snippet: Paris , the capital and largest city of France, is located in the northern part of the country on the banks of the Seine River . Including the surrounding metropolitan area, the population of Greater Paris reaches around 13 million people . Paris traces its roots back to the 3rd century BC when it was founded by the Celtic Parisii tribe on an island in the Seine River. During the Middle Ages , Paris grew into a significant religious and commercial center, witnessing the construction of Notre-Dame Cathedral in the 12th century and the founding of the University of Paris (Sorbonne) in the 13th century. In the 19th century, the city underwent major transformations under the supervision of Baron Haussmann , who redesigned its streets and infrastructure, giving Paris its modern architectural character. results.title: Paris facts: the capital of France in history
results.snippet: ## Paris facts: Paris, the **capital of France**. Paris is the **capital of France**, the largest country of Europe with 550 000 km2 (65 millions inhabitants). Paris has 2.234 million inhabitants end 2011. ## Paris facts: Paris history. From the Romans to the Vth Republic, Paris has a rich 2000 year history. See details of Paris history. ## Paris facts: Paris, a world city. Paris is a world capital city of shopping and fashion, with Channel, Dior, Vuitton, Yves Saint Laurent among many other top french fashion brands. ## Paris facts: the capital of France in history. Before Paris, the capital of France was Lyon (under the Romans). Paris first became the capital of France in 508 under King Clovis. After centuries with no unique capital of France, Paris retrieved its status of capital of France under King Philippe Auguste, who reigned between 1180 and 1223. Paris remained the capital of France until today, with one four year interruption.

results.title: Five interesting facts about Paris you need to know! - BBC Bitesize
results.snippet: Paris is the capital city of France and a cultural and culinary hub. The Louvre, a museum and art gallery in Paris, is the largest museum on earth with nearly 73,000 square metres of exhibition space - that’s more than the size of ten football pitches! The glass pyramid at the centre of the Louvre art gallery, Paris. People viewing the Mona Lisa within the Louvre art gallery. The cafés and coffeehouses of 18th century Paris provided a place for intellectual conversations where philosophers developed new ideas and theories. But the first people to present *projected* moving pictures to a paying audience, in anything like the cinemas we have today, were the Lumière brothers in December 1895 in Paris, France. The completed statue was presented to the American minister to France in Paris on 4th July, 1884. Paris is the second city to have hosted three summer Olympic Games. Paris previously hosted the Olympic Games in 1900 and 1924.
